solution:
def isSubstringPresent(self, s: str) -> bool:
    n = len(s)
    for i in range(n // 2):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            if s[i:j] == s[::-1][i:j]:
                return True
    return False

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We use nested loops to iterate through the string s and check if the substring from index i to j is present in the reversed string s[::-1][i:j]. Since the length of the input string is n, the time complexity is O(n^2). The space complexity is O(1) because we only use a constant amount of space for the nested loops. The energy implications are low because the algorithm only performs a few operations and does not consume significant energy.
